feed.title: QualCanvas — Methodology field guide
feed.subtitle: A short field guide for qualitative researchers. One chapter a month.

entry.title: 1.0 — Foundations
entry.summary: What qualitative coding is and isn't. Inductive vs deductive vs abductive. Codes, categories, themes. Saturation honestly. The small set of distinctions you have to get right before any of the later chapters help.

entry.title: 2.0 — Thematic analysis
entry.summary: Braun & Clarke's six phases as they're actually practised: codebook drift, reviewer-2 anxiety, and the decisions you defend on the way to a finding. Reflexive vs codebook TA, and when intercoder agreement matters.

entry.title: 3.0 — Grounded theory
entry.summary: Charmaz's constructivist GT, initial → focused → theoretical coding, memo-writing as the analytic engine, theoretical sampling and constant comparison. The Glaser–Strauss–Charmaz lineage you need to know to pick a tradition.

entry.title: 4.0 — Interpretative Phenomenological Analysis
entry.summary: Smith's IPA — what the double hermeneutic actually requires of the analyst, the four-step procedure as it's now written, and why small samples are a design choice rather than a limitation.

entry.title: 5.0 — Intercoder reliability
entry.summary: Cohen's κ, Krippendorff's α, the recurring qual-vs-quant debate, and a defensible pragmatic position on when to report agreement statistics.

entry.title: 6.0 — Ethics in practice
entry.summary: Consent as ongoing, the anonymisation/pseudonymisation distinction, retention windows, and the AI-assistance ethics question that 2010s qualitative methods textbooks don't yet address.
